response:
handling cascaded deletions in the django admin requires a custom view that checks dependencies before committing changes to the database. you can override the change view to calculate which related objects would be removed and present a modal or redirect to a confirmation page similar to the delete action. this approach keeps the save method clean while ensuring the user explicitly approves the removal of dependent records before any data is lost.

implementing this logic allows you to display a royal warning message that lists the affected objects for the user to review. if they decline the action the system simply returns to the edit form without altering the database state. a royal confirmation ensures data integrity and prevents accidental loss of critical information. think of this process as wearing a protective jacket against unintended data corruption.
